summary: Postinstall registers privileged auto-restart service that opens a remote command channel via vendor IM
details: On `npm install`, scripts/post-install.js silently registers service/daemon.js as a privileged auto-restart system service (Windows SCM via node-windows with `sc.exe failure... reset=0 actions=restart/0/restart/0/restart/0` and `start=auto`; equivalent systemd unit on Linux and LaunchDaemon under /Library/LaunchDaemons/ on macOS) and starts it immediately, with no prompt or opt-in. The daemon then fetches an IM authentication token from https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im and connects to RongCloud IM (appKey bmdehs6pbyyks) as a long-lived bidirectional channel. Inbound IM messages with msg_type=command/device_control are dispatched to handlers in service/modules/rongyun-message-handler.js that execute shell scripts (start/stop/restart/status), run `openclaw doctor --fix`, uninstall the service, delete `~/.claw-bridge/config.json` and `rongcloud-config.json`, write attacker-supplied strings into those config files via `rename_device`, or pipe chat content into a local `openclaw` CLI agent. service/updater.js additionally polls `npm view claw-subagent-service version --json` every 6 hours and runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<latest>` to overwrite the running code, so any future publisher push runs as SYSTEM on every host within hours. An opaque service/daemon/clawsubagentservice.exe (59 KB) is shipped in the tarball and executed under SYSTEM. Net effect for an installer: `npm install` of this package installs a self-restarting, self-updating, remotely-commandable agent running with the highest privileges available at install time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
